clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair centroid targeted by get_close_to_stair lies in a navmesh-disconnected component, so Reach_stair_centroid never becomes True and the agent never enters stair traversal. Floor switching is permanently blocked regardless of exploration depth or parameter tuning.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: both scenes show 27+ consecutive Reach_stair_centroid:False, pointnav cannot close the distance to the stair centroid, the stair frontier is disabled without any floor switch, and behavioral fingerprints are identical across candidate pairs — confirming no tested patch has reached the causal path. Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s targets an upstair centroid (~y=3.27, never enters is_robot_in_stair_map_fast evaluation) while qyAac8rV8Zk targets a downstair centroid (~y=-8.19, oscillating min_dis 160–166px with two explicit frontier-disable events). The disconnection manifests earlier in q3zU7Yy5E5s (approach is never initiated at stair-map level) vs. qyAac8rV8Zk (approach is attempted but pointnav oscillates at the navmesh boundary).
clusters.transfer_notes: The premature-stair-climb-success guard (candidate_1) was off-target for both scenes and produced identical fingerprints in both: it addresses stair-climb evaluation logic, which is never reached when the centroid is unreachable. The shared negative confirms that any fix must operate at or before the get_close_to_stair decision — i.e., the centroid reachability must be detected and bypassed before approach begins, not patched inside the stair-climb evaluator. The 75-step regression in q3zU7Yy5E5s from candidate_1 (381→456 steps) further shows that off-target patches can actively worsen episodes, so the fix must be precisely scoped to centroid reachability.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: stair_centroid_navmesh_reachability_precheck_before_get_close_to_stair: before entering get_close_to_stair, query the navmesh to verify the centroid shares the same connected component as the agent's current position; if disconnected, immediately invoke alternative_stair_entry_point_sampling_from_connected_navmesh_boundary (resample from the navmesh boundary nearest the stair polygon) or trigger early_abort_on_N_consecutive_centroid_failures_with_floor_skip to bypass the unreachable stair entirely

clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair is physically traversed successfully (floor_step resets to 0 at step 198), but the upper floor yields only two frontiers spanning 0.2–2.2m, exhausted in 16 steps; subsequent floor_reinits and NOQUIT rescues fail because the goal bed lies in a navmesh-disconnected region that no amount of re-exploration can bridge.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Distinguishing features: stair traversal is confirmed successful (unlike the stair_centroid_navmesh_unreachable cluster), the failure is post-switch rather than pre-switch, and the navmesh disconnection affects the goal region rather than the stair centroid. Five floor_reinits and two NOQUIT rescues all fail to generate usable frontiers near the bed, suggesting the upper-floor navmesh coverage around the goal is structurally insufficient.
clusters.transfer_notes: No direct cross-scene transfer within this cluster (single scene). From the other cluster: the navmesh-disconnection pattern recurs, but here it is the goal that is disconnected rather than the stair approach path. A navmesh connectivity precheck developed for the stair_centroid_navmesh_unreachable cluster could be repurposed as a navmesh_connectivity_precheck_for_floor_skip lever here — if the goal is confirmed unreachable after floor switch, the agent should skip the floor rather than exhaust reinits. The premature-stair-climb-success guard was also off-target here (SUCCESS fired at paused_step=20 > MIN_STAIR_STEPS=15), confirming the stair mechanics are not the lever.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: post_floor_switch_frontier_seeding_radius: after a confirmed floor switch, increase the frontier seeding radius to populate more of the new floor's navmesh, combined with DP10_DP11_value_map_upper_floor_coverage to inject semantic signal into unexplored regions; if frontier supply remains below a minimum threshold after N reinits, trigger navmesh_connectivity_precheck_for_floor_skip to detect and abandon the disconnected goal floor rather than looping through NOQUIT rescues

forbidden_moves.reason: Mandatory globally forbidden move; LLM response parsing has no causal path to stair centroid reachability or post-switch frontier supply
Mandatory globally forbidden move; LLM prompt content cannot affect navmesh connectivity or geometric reachability
Mandatory globally forbidden move; same reasoning as DP5
Mandatory globally forbidden move; LLM trigger threshold is upstream of and causally disconnected from stair/goal navmesh failures
Mandatory globally forbidden move; multi-floor LLM path cannot resolve geometric navmesh disconnection
Mandatory globally forbidden move; floor-switch interval gating is irrelevant when the stair centroid is never reached (cluster 1) or the floor switch already succeeded (cluster 2)

Mandatory globally forbidden move; empirically confirmed off-target for all three scenes — identical behavioral fingerprints before and after patch in every case, with a 75-step regression observed in q3zU7Yy5E5s

Ruled out for q3zU7Yy5E5s (stair-climb evaluation is never entered so carrot waypoint distance is never exercised); note DP9_stair_carrot_distance_for_downstair remains a candidate for qyAac8rV8Zk individually, but is not a cluster-wide lever
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE: stair was successfully traversed at paused_step=20, so stair carrot mechanics are confirmed not on the failure's causal path
Ruled out for qyAac8rV8Zk: frontier scoring cannot affect pointnav convergence to a geometrically unreachable centroid; not a cluster-wide lever since it was not ruled out for q3zU7Yy5E5s
